questions.question: Select the Venn diagram that best represents the relationship between the following classes. Task, Work, Cloud ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25487/image006.png)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25487/image007.png) So, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which word comes third when arranged in alphabetical order? magnet, magnate, magnetic, magneto, magnetism
questions.explanation: Sorted order → 1) magnet 2) magnate 3) magnetic 4) magnetism 5) magneto Therefore, the third word is magnetic.

questions.question: The sequence of folding a piece of paper and the manner in which the folded paper has been cut is shown in the following figures. Select the option that would most clearly resemble the unfolded form of paper. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28857/image034.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28857/image035.png)
questions.explanation: Figure I would resemble the correct form of a paper after cutting. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28857/image036.png)

questions.question: Select the term that breaks the pattern. 1, - 8, 27, - 65, 125, - 216, 343
questions.explanation: 1³ = 1 -2³ = -8 3³ = 27 -4³ = -64 [-65 breaks the pattern]

questions.question: From the relation below, determine which conclusion follows: Statement: H > I ≥ J > K = L Conclusions: I. J > L II. H = I
questions.explanation: Given statement: H > I ≥ J > K = L J > K = L. J > L. So, Conclusion I is true. H > I. So, Conclusion II is false. Hence, only conclusion I follows.

questions.question: Jai has the following profile: Education: B.Sc. 68% and M.Sc. in Chemistry (passed) Experience: 12 months full-time + 12 months part-time at 20 hours/week Age: 25 years Eligibility Criteria for the Position: Age: 24 - 30 years Experience: Minimum 2 years FULL-TIME equivalent; part-time counts at 50% credit Qualification: At least M.Sc. in Chemistry Decision: Based on the provided information, what is the most appropriate decision regarding Jai's eligibility for the position?
options: Candidate is not to be selected
Candidate is eligible only if he accepts a probationary role
questions.explanation: Full-time equivalent experience = 12 months + (12 X 50%) months = 12 + 6 = 18 months. Required full-time equivalent = 24 months. Experience deficit persists; other criteria cannot compensate. Decision => not to be selected.

questions.question: If 'x' means '+', '+' means 'x', '-' means '÷' and '÷' means '-', then what will come in the place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? 54 ÷ 18 - 6 x 4 + 5 = ?

Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits.) 317 : 4461 :: 589 : 8269 :: 432 : ?
A stack contains 81 boxes. Cookies box is 31ˢᵗ from the top, and Nuts box is 20ᵗʰ from the bottom. If Spices box is kept immediately above Nuts box. What is the position of Spices box from the top?
questions.explanation: A stack contains 81 boxes. Cookies box is 31ˢᵗ from the top, and Nuts box is 20ᵗʰ from the bottom. If Spices box is kept immediately above Nuts box. 30 boxes Cookies box(31ˢᵗ from the top) 'N' Spices box Nuts box (20ᵗʰ from the bottom) 19 boxes N = 81 - [30 + 1(Cookies) + 1(Spices) + 1(Nuts) + 19] N = 81 - (30+1+1+1+19) N = 81 - 52 N = 29 Boxes above Spices box = 30 + 1 + 29 = 60 Thus, Spices box is 61ˢᵗ form the top.

questions.question: Find the missing number: ? : 1320 :: 8 : 504
questions.explanation: The relation follows the pattern n³ − n. For n = 8: 8³ − 8 = 512 − 8 = 504. Using the same rule for n = 11: 11³ − 11 = 1331 − 11 = 1320.

questions.question: Find the word that is different from the rest.
questions.explanation: Carpenter, Plumber and Electrician are skilled trades. Dentist is a healthcare profession. Therefore, Dentist is the odd one out.

questions.question: In a certain code language, "Plan implement achieve" is coded as "Y1Ω H3% Q2≤", "Implement track success" is coded as "F4∆ Q2≤ T5+" and "Achieve track results" is coded as "H3% F4∆ W6~". What will be 'Results' coded in that language?
questions.explanation: In a certain code language, "Plan implement achieve" is coded as "Y1Ω H3% Q2≤", "Implement track success" is coded as "F4∆ Q2≤ T5+" "Achieve track results" is coded as "H3% F4∆ W6~". The code for 'Implement' = Q2≤ The code for 'Track' = F4∆ The code for 'Achieve' = H3% Thus, the code for 'Results' = W6~

questions.question: Study the following information and answer the given question: 'Y ® Z' means Z is the brother of Y. 'Y * Z' means Z is the mother of Y. 'Y & Z' means Z is the father of Y. 'Y % Z' means Z is the son of Y. 'Y $ Z' means Z is the sister of Y. How is S related to G in the given expression S * T $ V & K % L ® G?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30420/image027.jpg) It is given that, 'Y ® Z' means Z is the brother of Y. 'Y * Z' means Z is the mother of Y. 'Y & Z' means Z is the father of Y. 'Y % Z' means Z is the son of Y. 'Y $ Z' means Z is the sister of Y. After decoding the given expression, we get: S * T $ V & K % L ® G T is the mother of S. V is the sister of T. K is the father of V. L is the son of K. G is the brother of L. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30420/image028.jpg) So, S is either niece or nephew of G.

questions.question: There are seven members in a family. H is the mother of Y. G is married to T. T is the sister of Y. L is the father-in-law of Y. A is the daughter of L. S is the father of T. How is A related to S?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image001.jpg) There are seven members in a family. H is the mother of Y. G is married to T. T is the sister of Y. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image002.jpg) L is the father-in-law of Y. A is the daughter of L. S is the father of T. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image003.jpg) So, A is the daughter-in-law of S.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 3, 4, 8, 35, 51, 176, ?
Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. KBF, LDI, MFL, NHO, OJR, ?
questions.explanation: Here, the logic is: For the 1ˢᵗ letter of each term: K+1= L; L+1= M; M+1= N; N+1=O; O+1= P For the 2ⁿᵈ letter of each term: B+2= D; D+2= F; F+2= H; H+2= J; J+2= L For the 3ʳᵈ letter of each term: F+3= I; I+3= L; L+3= O; O+3= R; R+3= U

questions.question: There are 'X' number of students in a classroom. 51 persons speak English, 26 students speak Hindi and 12 students speak both English and Hindi. All the students speak atleast one of the two languages. How many students are there in a classroom?
questions.explanation: There are 'X' number of students in a classroom. English speaking students = 51 Hindi speaking students = 26 English and Hindi speaking students = 12 Also, all the students speak atleast one of the two languages. Thus, Total number of students = English speaking students + Hindi speaking students - English and Hindi speaking students = 51 + 26 - 12 = 65

questions.question: What comes next in the series? G, H, L, U, K, ?
questions.explanation: G + 1 = H H + 4 = L L + 9 = U U + 16 = K K + 25 = J So, the next term in the series is J.

questions.question: Read the following statement carefully and identify the assumption(s) that is/are implicit. Statement: The university decided to conduct open-book exams in selected subjects to discourage rote learning. Assumptions: I. Open-book exams reward understanding more than memorisation in those subjects. II. Open-book exams will completely stop students from sharing answers during exams.
options: Both Assumption I and Assumption II are implicit
Neither Assumption I nor Assumption II is implicit
questions.explanation: Statement: The university decided to conduct open-book exams in selected subjects to discourage rote learning. This indicates that the university believes this method will promote understanding and reduce reliance on memorisation. Assumption I: The decision to implement open-book exams is based on the belief that they evaluate comprehension and application rather than mere memorisation. Without this assumption, the intended purpose of discouraging rote learning would not be served. Therefore, this assumption is implicit. Assumption II: The statement does not assume total prevention of answer-sharing. The goal is to discourage rote learning, not to guarantee complete elimination of malpractice. Hence, this assumption is not implicit. So, only Assumption I is implicit.

questions.question: If BRISK = 54 and GARDEN = 43, then what is the value of UNITY?
questions.explanation: A (1)B (2)C (3)D (4)E (5)F (6)G (7)H (8)I (9)J (10)K (11)L (12)M (13) Z (26)Y (25)X (24)W (23)V (22)U (21)T (20)S (19)R (18)Q (17)P (16)O (15)N (14) BRISK: 2+18+9+19+11 = 59 Number of Letters = 5 59 − 5 = 54 GARDEN: 7+1+18+4+5+14 = 49 Number of Letters = 6 49 − 6 = 43 So, value = (sum of letter values) − (Number of Letters) UNITY: 21+14+9+20+25 = 89 Number of Letters = 5 89 − 5 = 84

questions.question: Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: A company is considering introducing flexible working hours for its employees. Conclusions: I. Flexible working hours will definitely start from next Monday. II. At present, the company does not allow any flexibility in working hours.
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The statement only says the company is considering the idea, so it does not confirm implementation on any specific date. The statement also does not describe the current working-hour policy, so it cannot be concluded that there is no flexibility at present.

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series, will complete the series. h _ t c _ n _ h i _ c g _ k _ i _ c _ n k
questions.explanation: Option (a): gikntthg h g t c i n k h i n c g t k t i h c g n k. No pattern is formed. Option (b): igktnhtg hitcgnk hitcgnk hitcgnk. 'hitcgnk' is repeated thrice. Option (c): tigkhtgn h t t c i n g h i k c g h k t i g c n n k. No pattern is formed. Option (d): ikgnthgt h i t c k n g h i n c g t k h i g c t n k. No pattern is formed. Thus, the answer is option (b).

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 5040, ?, 168, 42, 14, 7
Based on the following statements, which is the correct conclusion drawn? Statements: No golden jubilee is silver jubilee. All silver jubilees are platinum jubilees.
options: Some golden jubilee is platinum jubilee
No platinum jubilee is golden jubilee
All platinum jubilee is silver jubilee
Some platinum jubilee are not golden jubilee
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p18392/image026.jpg) No golden jubilee is silver jubilee. So, the golden jubilee which are not silver jubilee can be platinum jubilee. So, option (a) is incorrect. The platinum jubilee which are not silver jubilee can be golden jubilee. So, option (b) is incorrect. All silver jubilees are platinum jubilees which means some platinum jubilee is silver jubilee. So, option (c) is incorrect. No golden jubilee is silver jubilee and all silver jubilees are platinum jubilees. Thus, some platinum jubilee are not golden jubilee. So, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Schedule VII of the Indian Constitution, which provides for the division of legislative powers between the Union and the States, is the legal instrument that gives effect to which specific Article?
questions.explanation: Article 246 deals with the subject-matter of laws made by Parliament and State Legislatures. It distributes legislative powers between the Union and the States by referring to the three lists in Schedule VII: Union List State List Concurrent List Article 244 deals with administration of Scheduled Areas and Tribal Areas. Article 248 gives residuary powers of legislation to Parliament. Article 250 allows Parliament to legislate on State List subjects during a National Emergency. Further Insights The 42nd Constitutional Amendment shifted some subjects from State List to Concurrent List (e.g., Education, Forests). Residuary powers in India lie with the Union, unlike the USA where they lie with States. In case of conflict on Concurrent List subjects, Union law prevails (Article 254). Article 246A (added by 101st Amendment) gives special power regarding GST legislation.

questions.question: With reference to the use of Baking Soda (Sodium Bicarbonate) in the food industry, consider the following statements: i) Baking soda releases Carbon Dioxide gas when it reacts with acidic ingredients or is subjected to heat, which helps the dough to rise. ii) The soft and spongy texture of a cake is primarily a result of the entrapment of oxygen bubbles released during the thermal decomposition of Sodium Bicarbonate. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is Correct Baking soda is Sodium Bicarbonate (NaHCO₃). When heated or when it reacts with an acid (like vinegar, lemon juice, buttermilk), it releases Carbon Dioxide (CO₂). The CO₂ gas forms bubbles in the dough. These bubbles expand during baking, making the dough rise. Statement (ii) is Incorrect The gas released is Carbon Dioxide (CO₂), not oxygen. Oxygen is not produced during thermal decomposition of Sodium Bicarbonate. The reaction on heating is: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30509/image001.gif) No oxygen is formed. Further Insights Baking powder contains baking soda + a weak acid + starch. Yeast also produces CO₂, but through fermentation. Sodium Carbonate (Na₂CO₃) formed after heating is alkaline. Too much baking soda makes food taste bitter. CO₂ expansion increases with temperature, improving rise during baking.

questions.question: The 'Hojagiri' folk dance, characterized by dancers balancing on earthen pitchers and moving only the lower half of their bodies, is traditionally performed by which of the following communities?
questions.explanation: Hojagiri is a famous folk dance of the Reang (Bru) community. It is mainly performed in Tripura. The dance is unique because: Performers balance earthen pitchers, bottles, or lamps on their heads. They move only the lower half of their bodies, keeping the upper body steady. Further Insights Reang community is also known as the Bru tribe. Hojagiri is usually performed by young girls. The dance emphasizes balance and body control. Tripura has several tribal dance traditions linked to harvest festivals.

questions.question: According to the 'Normal Lapse Rate', for every how many meters of height gained on a mountain does the temperature drop by 1°C?
questions.explanation: In the troposphere (the lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere), temperature generally decreases with an increase in altitude. This phenomenon is known as the Normal Lapse Rate. Understanding the Normal Lapse Rate The standard figures used for this rate are: · 1°C for every 165 meters of ascent. · 6.5°C for every 1,000 meters (1 km) of ascent. Why does this happen? · Atmospheric Pressure: As you go higher, the air pressure decreases. When air rises, it expands, and as it expands, it loses heat energy, causing the temperature to drop. · Distance from Heat Source: The atmosphere is primarily heated from below by the Earth's surface (which absorbs solar radiation and radiates it back as long-wave terrestrial radiation). As you move further away from the surface, the heating effect diminishes. · Air Density: High-altitude air is less dense and contains fewer greenhouse gases (like water vapor and $CO_2$) to trap heat compared to air at sea level. It is worth noting that this rate is "normal" but not "constant." Sometimes, a phenomenon called Temperature Inversion occurs, where the temperature actually increases with height for a short distance (often seen in valleys during winter nights). Further Insights Temperature decreases with altitude due to decreasing air pressure. Hill stations are cooler because of lapse rate effect. Temperature inversion is the opposite of lapse rate. Lapse rate affects cloud formation and rainfall. DALR is higher than SALR because condensation releases latent heat.

questions.question: According to the Laws of the Game in Association Football, what is the required radius of the center circle where the game commences?
questions.explanation: The radius of the center circle in football is 9.15 metres, which equals 10 yards. This distance ensures that during a kick-off: All opposing players remain at least 9.15 m away from the ball The team taking the kick-off has proper space Further Insights The distance for a defensive wall at free kicks is also 9.15 m (10 yards). A standard football field length ranges from 90 - 120 metres (international: 100 - 110 m). The Laws of the Game are governed by IFAB (International Football Association Board).

questions.question: According to Article 266 of the Indian Constitution, which of the following is NOT classified as a part of the 'Public Account of India'?
questions.explanation: Article 266 of the Constitution of India provides for: Consolidated Fund of India Public Account of India Corporate Tax is a revenue receipt of the Government of India. All revenues received by Government of India All loans raised All money received in repayment of loans Therefore, Corporate Tax goes into the Consolidated Fund, not the Public Account. Further Insights Article 267 provides for the Contingency Fund of India. Public Account funds are liabilities of the government. CAG audits both Consolidated Fund and Public Account. Borrowings of the government also go into the Consolidated Fund.

questions.question: With reference to the Numbeo Safety Index 2026, consider the following statements: i) Mangaluru has been identified as the safest city in India, achieving a global rank of 46 with a safety score of 74.4. ii) At the national level, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) is ranked as the safest country in the world, while India holds the 70th position. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are factually accurate according to the 2026 report. Statement 1 correctly identifies Mangaluru (Karnataka) as the top-ranked Indian city. Statement 2 accurately reflects the global country rankings where the UAE leads with a score of 86.0 and India follows at rank 70 with a score of 55.8. Further Insights The Numbeo Safety Index is a crowd-sourced database, meaning it relies on the perceptions and reports of residents and visitors rather than just official police records. Qingdao, China, has been ranked as the safest city in the entire world for the year 2026 with a score of 89.2. The index factors in various safety dimensions, such as the fear of being mugged or attacked and the safety of walking alone at night. Apart from Mangaluru, other Indian cities in the top five include Ahmedabad, Jaipur, Coimbatore, and Thiruvananthapuram. The United Arab Emirates (UAE) dominates the top global city rankings, with Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, and Dubai all featuring in the world's top five safest cities.

questions.question: Which city has been selected to host the inaugural edition of the ICC Women's Emerging Nations Trophy in November 2025?
questions.explanation: The inaugural ICC Women's Emerging Nations Trophy (T20I) for November 2025 is scheduled to be held in Bangkok, Thailand. The tournament is a new ICC initiative involving eight Associate member women's teams and will take place from 20 - 30 November 2025 with matches hosted in Bangkok. Further Insights The ICC Women's Emerging Nations Trophy is part of a new development strategy to grow women's cricket globally. Eight Associate member teams will compete in a round-robin format. Matches will be played at multiple grounds in Bangkok, including the Terdthai Cricket Ground. Thailand - as the host - also fields a competing team. This event helps bridge the gap between full member and Associate women's teams in international cricket.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labeled as Assertion (A) and the other as Reason (R): Assertion (A): In a ballpoint pen, gravity and capillary action work together to ensure a steady flow of ink onto the paper through a rotating ball at the tip. Reason (R): The ink in the reservoir tube remains stable and does not leak out because ballpoint pen ink has zero surface tension, allowing it to move freely regardless of the tube's width. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is True A ballpoint pen works due to: Gravity (helps ink move downward) Capillary action (helps ink flow through narrow spaces) Rolling action of the tiny ball at the tip, which transfers ink onto paper The rotating ball controls the ink flow and prevents excessive leakage. Reason (R) is False The statement says ink has zero surface tension - this is scientifically incorrect. Ballpoint ink actually has high viscosity and non-zero surface tension. Surface tension is important to prevent leakage. If surface tension were zero, the ink would leak uncontrollably. Further Insights Capillary action depends on both surface tension and adhesion. Ballpoint pens use thick oil-based ink. Fountain pens rely more on capillary action than ballpoint pens. Surface tension decreases with increasing temperature. The rolling ball acts as both a valve and an applicator.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labeled as Assertion (A) and the other as Reason (R). Assertion (A): Real GDP is considered a superior indicator of a nation's actual economic growth compared to Nominal GDP. Reason (R): Nominal GDP is calculated using constant prices from a fixed base year, thereby eliminating the distorting effects of inflation on the final value. Which of the following is correct?
options: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Why Assertion (A) is True Real GDP is calculated using constant prices (base year prices). It removes the impact of inflation. Therefore, it reflects the actual increase in production of goods and services. Why Reason (R) is False The statement says Nominal GDP is calculated using constant prices,That is incorrect. Nominal GDP is calculated using current market prices. It includes the effect of inflation. Further Insights Real GDP = Nominal GDP adjusted using GDP Deflator. GDP Deflator = (Nominal GDP / Real GDP) X 100. Nominal GDP is useful for comparing tax revenues and fiscal ratios. High inflation widens the gap between Nominal and Real GDP.

questions.question: During the celebration of the Nuakhai harvest festival, the first grain of the season is traditionally offered to which presiding deity?
questions.explanation: Nuakhai is a major harvest festival of Western Odisha. The word "Nuakhai" literally means "eating the new rice." Traditionally, the first grain of the newly harvested crop is offered to Maa Samaleswari, the presiding deity of Sambalpur region. She is regarded as the guardian deity of Western Odisha. Lord Jagannath Lord Jagannath is mainly associated with Puri and the famous Rath Yatra. Goddess Lakshmi Although Lakshmi is the goddess of wealth and prosperity, Lord Lingaraj Lord Lingaraj is associated with Bhubaneswar and the Lingaraj Temple. Further Insights Nuakhai is celebrated a day after Ganesh Chaturthi. It is mainly observed in Western Odisha and parts of Chhattisgarh. The festival strengthens community bonding and family ties. It marks the beginning of the new agricultural cycle. Maa Samaleswari Temple is located in Sambalpur.

questions.question: What is the name of the process in the nephron where essential substances like glucose and amino acids are taken back into the blood from the filtered fluid?
questions.explanation: In the nephron, blood is first filtered in the glomerulus. The filtered fluid (called filtrate) contains: Water Glucose Amino acids Salts Urea In the proximal convoluted tubule (PCT), essential substances like: Glucose Amino acids Certain ions are reabsorbed back into the blood. Filtration Filtration occurs in the glomerulus. It is the process of filtering blood to form filtrate. Secretion Secretion is the movement of additional wastes (like hydrogen ions, potassium, drugs) from blood into the tubule. Excretion Excretion is the final removal of urine from the body. Further Insights About 99% of filtrate is reabsorbed. Loop of Henle helps concentrate urine. ADH hormone regulates water reabsorption. Each kidney contains about 1 million nephrons.

questions.question: Which judicial body, established under the Government of India Act, 1935, is considered the direct predecessor of the Supreme Court of India?
questions.explanation: The Federal Court of India was established in 1937 under the Government of India Act, 1935. It had: Original jurisdiction, Appellate jurisdiction, Advisory jurisdiction In 1950, it was replaced by the Supreme Court of India after the Constitution came into force. Further Insights The Federal Court started functioning in 1937. Appeals from the Federal Court could still go to the Privy Council until 1949. The Supreme Court of India was established on 26 January 1950. Article 124 of the Constitution provides for the Supreme Court. The first Chief Justice of India was H.J. Kania.

questions.question: Saina Nehwal, the first Indian woman badminton player to win an Olympic medal, secured the Bronze medal at the Olympics in which year?
questions.explanation: Saina Nehwal won the Bronze medal at the 2012 London Olympics. She became the first Indian woman badminton player to win an Olympic medal. She was awarded the bronze after her opponent, Wang Xin of China, retired due to injury during the bronze medal match. Saina Nehwal Retires from Badminton · Career span: 21 years · Former World No.1 (2015) · Olympic Bronze: London 2012 · First Indian woman badminton Olympic medallist. Awards · Padma Shri - 2010 · Padma Bhushan - 2016 · Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna - 2010 · Arjuna Award - 2009 Further Insights P.V. Sindhu later won Silver (2016) and Bronze (2020 Tokyo) in Olympics. Saina became World No. 1 in 2015. She won Gold at the 2010 Commonwealth Games. Badminton is governed globally by the BWF (Badminton World Federation). The 2012 London Olympics was India's then-best Olympic performance (6 medals).

questions.question: The economic strategy of 'Import Substitution', which aimed to replace foreign imports with domestic production to achieve self-reliance, was a hallmark of which Indian Five-Year Plan?
questions.explanation: The Second Five-Year Plan (1956 - 61) emphasized rapid industrialization. It was based on the Mahalanobis Model. The focus was on: Heavy industries Capital goods sector Reducing dependence on imports This approach is known as Import Substitution Industrialization (ISI). First Five-Year Plan Period: 1951 - 56 Focus: Agriculture, irrigation, and food security Fourth Five-Year Plan Period: 1969 - 74 Focus: Growth with stability and self-reliance Sixth Five-Year Plan Period: 1980 - 85 Focus: Poverty alleviation and technological modernization Further Insights The Second Plan was heavily influenced by economist P.C. Mahalanobis. ISI led to long-term industrial base development but also inefficiencies. India shifted away from strict import substitution after the 1991 Economic Reforms.

questions.question: Under the Jain philosophy of Right Conduct (Charitra), which of the following is NOT one of the 'Pancha Mahavratas' (Five Great Vows) prescribed for attaining liberation?
questions.explanation: Samavara is not one of the Pancha Mahavratas. Samavara refers to the stoppage of karmic influx - meaning preventing new karma from attaching to the soul. It is an important Jain philosophical concept, but it is not one of the Five Great Vows. The Five Great Vows are: Ahimsa - Non-violence Satya - Truthfulness Asteya - Non-stealing Brahmacharya - Celibacy Aparigraha - Non-possession Further Insights The Five Great Vows are strictly followed by Jain monks, while laypersons follow them in limited form (Anuvratas). Jainism emphasizes extreme non-violence (Ahimsa) as its highest principle. The Three Jewels (Triratna) are Right Faith, Right Knowledge, and Right Conduct. Samavara and Nirjara are concepts related to karma theory. Jainism rejects the authority of the Vedas.

questions.question: 'Nomadic Elephant' is a recurring bilateral military exercise conducted annually between the Indian Army and the army of which other nation?
questions.explanation: Exercise Nomadic Elephant is a bilateral military exercise between India and Mongolia. It is conducted annually and focuses on counter-insurgency and counter-terrorism operations in semi-urban and mountainous terrain. The exercise alternates between India and Mongolia. Thailand India conducts military exercises with Thailand (such as MAITREE) Kyrgyzstan India conducts Exercise Khanjar with Kyrgyzstan. Further Insights Mongolia is strategically important to India under its "Act East Policy." The exercise enhances interoperability in UN peacekeeping-style operations. Military exercises strengthen diplomatic ties beyond just defence cooperation.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labeled as Assertion (A) and the other as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The Nayankara system was a fundamental pillar of the Vijayanagara Empire's military and civil administration. Reason (R): The King (Raya) granted land territories called 'Amarams' to the Nayakas in exchange for military service and a share of the annual land revenue. Which of the following is correct regarding the statements above?
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true The Nayankara system was central to the Vijayanagara administrative structure. Nayakas were military chiefs who: Maintained troops, Collected revenue, Administered local territories This made the system both military and administrative in nature. Reason (R) is true The king granted land assignments known as Amarams to the Nayakas. In return, the Nayakas: Provided military service and paid a portion of revenue to the king This arrangement explains how the system functioned. Why (R) correctly explains (A) The Nayankara system became the backbone of the empire precisely because: It decentralized administration, It ensured a ready military force and It maintained revenue flow So the grant of Amarams in exchange for service is exactly why it was so important. Further Insights The Nayankara system had similarities with the Iqta system of the Delhi Sultanate. Nayakas often became powerful and semi-independent over time. Krishnadevaraya strengthened the Nayankara system. The system contributed to both expansion and later fragmentation of the empire. Vijayanagara was founded in 1336 by Harihara and Bukka.

questions.question: Under the Bharatiya SakshyaAdhiniyam (BSA), which has replaced the Indian Evidence Act of 1872, the colonial-era term "lunatic" has been substituted with which modern expression?
questions.explanation: Under the Bharatiya SakshyaAdhiniyam (BSA), the outdated and colonial-era term "lunatic" has been replaced with the legally appropriate and modern expression "person of unsound mind." This aligns the terminology with: Contemporary legal standards Respectful and neutral language Existing usage in Indian jurisprudence The phrase "person of unsound mind" is already widely used in Indian laws and court interpretations. Further Insights BSA formally recognizes electronic and digital evidence. It is part of India's broader criminal law reform of 2023. The Act aims to remove colonial vocabulary from the statute book. Many structural provisions remain similar to the 1872 Act but with modernization. Legal terminology must be precise because courts interpret words strictly.

questions.question: With reference to the Scramjet (Supersonic Combusting Ramjet) engine technology being developed by ISRO, consider the following statements: i) Unlike traditional rockets that carry both fuel and oxidizer, a Scramjet engine uses atmospheric oxygen as an oxidizer during its flight through the atmosphere. ii) The Scramjet engine is designed to operate efficiently only at subsonic speeds, making it ideal for low-altitude transport aircraft but not for space launch vehicles. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement i is Correct Traditional rockets carry both fuel and oxidizer because space has no oxygen. A Scramjet engine uses oxygen from the atmosphere for combustion. That means it doesn't need to carry oxidizer, reducing weight. Statement ii is Incorrect Scramjet stands for Supersonic Combusting Ramjet. It operates at supersonic and hypersonic speeds, not subsonic. It is specifically designed for very high-speed flight, including potential space launch systems. Further Insights ISRO successfully tested its Scramjet engine in 2016. Scramjets are part of reusable launch vehicle (RLV) research. They reduce launch cost by cutting oxidizer weight.

questions.question: With reference to the Dharwar Rock System in India, which of the following best describes its economic importance?
options: It mainly contains coal deposits used for thermal power generation.
It contains important metallic minerals like iron ore, gold and manganese.
It forms the Deccan Traps and is responsible for black soil formation.
It mainly provides marble and sandstone for construction.
questions.explanation: The Dharwar Rock System is one of the oldest rock systems in India. It consists of ancient metamorphosed sedimentary rocks and is very rich in metallic minerals such as: Iron ore Gold (Kolar Gold Fields) Manganese These minerals play a major role in India's industrial development, especially in steel production.

questions.question: David Szalay, a Canadian-Hungarian-British author, has been awarded the prestigious 2025 Booker Prize for which of the following novels?
questions.explanation: The 2025 Booker Prize was awarded to David Szalay for his novel Flesh. The Booker Prize is one of the world's most prestigious literary awards for fiction in English. Further Insights The Booker Prize recognizes the best original novel written in English and published in the UK or Ireland. David Szalay became the first Hungarian-British author to win the Booker Prize. Flesh explores the life of a man from Hungary to London in a sparse and compelling narrative. The prize carries a ≤50,000 award and global literary recognition. Szalay's earlier works, like All That Man Is, helped build his reputation before this win.

questions.question: With reference to the ancient Nalanda University, consider the following statements: i) The university's library, known as Dharmaganja (Mountain of Truth), was one of the largest repositories of Buddhist and secular knowledge in the ancient world. ii) It consisted of three massive multi-storeyed buildings named Ratnasagara, Ratnodadhi, and Ratnaranjaka. iii) The library contained hundreds of thousands of manuscripts covering subjects such as grammar, logic, astronomy and medicine. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Nalanda's library was called Dharmaganja, meaning "Treasury of Truth."( Mountain of Truth) It was one of the greatest centers of learning in the ancient world. It housed Buddhist scriptures as well as secular subjects. The library complex had three grand buildings: Ratnasagara, Ratnodadhi, Ratnaranjaka These were multi-storeyed and architecturally impressive. The library reportedly had hundreds of thousands of manuscripts. Subjects included: Grammar, Logic, Astronomy, Medicine, Philosophy This shows Nalanda was a multidisciplinary university. Further Insights Nalanda was established during the Gupta period (5th century CE). It flourished under Harsha and the Pala rulers. Xuanzang studied at Nalanda for several years. The university was destroyed in the 12th century by Bakhtiyar Khilji. It is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: With reference to the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan, consider the following statements: i) The platform utilizes a Geographic Information System (GIS) based digital interface developed by BISAG-N, integrating over 200 layers of geospatial data including forest land and industrial estates. ii) The PM Gati Shakti digital platform is primarily managed and was developed by the National Informatics Centre (NIC) to provide real-time satellite imagery for military logistics only. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: PM Gati Shakti is built as a GIS-based digital platform, and it integrates 200+ layers of geospatial data such as: Forest land Industrial corridors Railways Highways Ports Telecom infrastructure Power networks The platform was technically developed by BISAG-N (Bhaskaracharya National Institute for Space Applications and Geo-informatics). It says it was developed by NIC → Wrong. It was developed by BISAG-N, not NIC. It says it provides real-time satellite imagery for military logistics only → Completely wrong. PM Gati Shakti is for integrated infrastructure planning across ministries. It is not a defence-only or military-only platform. Further Insights PM Gati Shakti was launched in October 2021. It aims to achieve the vision of "Integrated and Holistic Infrastructure Development." It supports the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP). The plan promotes multimodal connectivity (road, rail, air, waterways). It reduces project delays by improving inter-ministerial coordination.

questions.question: Which wildlife sanctuary, was recently declared as an Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ) by the Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change (MoEFCC)?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) has officially notified the area around Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary as an Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ). This sanctuary is located in the Aravalli Range in the Indian state of Rajasthan. Further Insights Eco-Sensitive Zones (ESZs) act as buffers around protected areas to regulate human activities and reduce ecological damage. ESZ notification is part of India's broader environmental protection strategy under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986. Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary is notable for species like leopard, striped hyena, chinkara, Indian pangolin, and painted francolin. The Aravalli mountain range, where Kumbhalgarh lies, is one of the oldest and ecologically fragile ranges in India. Declaring ESZs often involves curbing mining, industrial activities, and construction near ecologically sensitive regions.

questions.question: Raghav has 30 litres of a coolant that is 20% antifreeze. He adds x litres of a 50% antifreeze coolant and then adds 5 litres of water. The final mixture is 25% antifreeze. Find x.
questions.explanation: Antifreeze balance equation: Initial antifreeze + Added antifreeze = Final antifreeze 0.20 X 30 + 0.50 X x + 0 X 5 = 0.25 X (30 + x + 5) 6 + 0.50x = 0.25 X (35 + x) 6 + 0.50x = 8.75 + 0.25x 0.50x - 0.25x = 8.75 - 6 0.25x = 2.75 x = 11 litres

questions.question: Parul began a business with Rs.4500 and was joined afterward by Neha with Rs.5400. If the profits at the end of the year were divided by the ratio of 2: 1. After how much time Neha joined the business?
questions.explanation: Let Neha joined the business for X months. profit ratio = 2: 1 4500 * 12/ (5400 * X) = 2/1 X = 5 So, Neha joined after 12 - 5 = 7 months

questions.question: If a person invested a certain sum in a compound interest at a 40% rate of interest compounded annually, then the compound interest received after two years is Rs. 1920. Find the sum invested.
questions.explanation: 40% = ⅖ C.I = P * (7ᵗ - 5ᵗ)/5ᵗ [7 = sum of fraction ⅖ and T = Time] 1920 = P * (7² - 5²)/5² P = 1920 * 25/24 = Rs. 2000

questions.question: ABCD is a rectangle whose three vertices are A (2, 4), B (2, 0), and C (7, 0). The length of its diagonal is:
questions.explanation: Length of its diagonal = Distance between AC = √[(7 - 2)² + (0 - 4)²] = √[25 + 16] = √41

questions.question: A cart contains two type of circular wheels of area 2464 cm² and 1386 cm² respectively, then what is the difference between total rounds made by both wheels if the cart travels for 7.92 km?
questions.explanation: Area of bigger wheel = (22/7) * R² = 2464 R² = 784 R = 28 cm Perimeter of bigger wheel = 2 * (22/7) * 28 = 176 cm Area of smaller wheel = (22/7) * r² = 1386 r² = 441 r = 21 cm Perimeter of smaller wheel = 2 * (22/7) * 21 = 132 cm Total rounds made by bigger wheel = 792000/176 = 4500 Total rounds made by smaller wheel = 792000/132 = 6000 Required difference = 6000 - 4500 = 1500

questions.question: A sum earns simple interest equal to one-ninth of itself when invested for a time (in years) numerically equal to the rate of interest (in % per annum). Find the rate.
questions.explanation: Let P = principal, R = rate (%), T = time (years), and R = T. Simple interest = P/9 Also SI = P·R·T/100 = PR²/100 So PR²/100 = P/9 => R² = 100/9 => R = 10/3 = 3.33%

questions.question: The area of ∆XYZ is 330 cm². Find the measure of YZ. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24581/image003.jpg)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24581/image004.jpg) Angle subtended by the diameter at any point on the circumference of the circle is 90°. ∠XYZ = 90° Area of ∆XYZ = 330 cm² ½ * XY * YZ = 330 60 * YZ = 660 YZ = 11 cm

questions.question: In a board examination, 80% of boys passed while 72% of girls passed. If there were 250 girls and the total number of failed candidates was 130, how many boys were there?
questions.explanation: Let B = number of boys. Boys failed = 20% of B = 0.20B. Girls failed = 28% of 250 = 70. Total failed = 0.20B + 70 = 130 => 0.20B = 60 => B = 300.

questions.question: Evaluate the value of p = 7 X {20 − [12 − (5 − 3) X 4]}.
questions.explanation: Use BODMAS: Brackets, then Multiplication/Division, then Addition/Subtraction. Let p = 7.{20 − [12 − (5 − 3).4]}. => (5 − 3).4 = 2.4 = 8 => [12 − 8] = 4 => {20 − 4} = 16 => p = 7 X 16 = 112

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys an item for ₹900. He plans to allow a 10% discount on its marked price but still earn a profit of 20% on cost. What should be the marked price?
questions.explanation: We know that, Marked Price/Cost Price = (100 + Profit%)/(100 - discount%) Marked Price/900 = 120/90 Marked Price = Rs.1200

questions.question: A solid sphere of surface area 5544 cm² is melted and with one-third of its volume total 'X' similar cones are made while from the remaining volume total 'Y' similar cylinders are made. What is the ratio of 'X' to 'Y' if base radius of each cone and cylinder is 7 cm while height of each cone and each cylinder is 21 cm and 8 cm respectively.
questions.explanation: Let radius of sphere = R Surface area of sphere = 4πR² = 5544 R² = (5544 * 7)/(22 * 4) R = 21 cm Volume of sphere = (4/3)πR³ Total volume of 'X' cones = (⅓) * (4/3)π(21)³ = X * (⅓)π * (7)² * (21) X = 12 Total volume of 'Y' cylinders = (⅔) * (4/3)π(21)³ = Y * π * (7)² * (8) Y = 21 Required ratio = X: Y = 12: 21 = 4: 7

questions.question: A quarter circle of area 4π cm² is folded along the straight edges to form a cone. What is the radius of the sphere whose volume is same as the cone?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p4967/image005.jpg) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p4967/image006.jpg) When a sector of a circle is folded in a cone, the radius of the circle becomes equal to the slant height of the cone and the arc length becomes equal to the circumference of the base. Let the radius of the circle(or slant height of the cone) be 'l' cm. (¼)πl² = 4π => l = 4 Let the radius of the cone be 'r' cm 2πr = (¼)*2πl => r = l/4 = 1 Height of the cone = √(l² - r²) = √(4² - 1²) = √15 cm Volume of cone = (⅓)π(1)²√15 = π√15/3 cm³ Let the radius of the sphere be 'R' cm ∴ (4/3)πR³ = π√15/3 => R = (15/16)¹/⁶

questions.question: A shopkeeper sold similar article to his two customers A and B. To customer A he allows, two successive discounts of 10% and 20% respectively while to customer B he allows, two successive discounts of 15% each. The difference between the selling price of article B to A is Rs.4. If the marked up per cent of the article is 60% above its cost price, then what is the profit amount earned by shopkeeper when he sold the article to customer B?
questions.explanation: Let the marked price of the article = 100x Selling price of article to customer A after two successive discounts = 90% of 80% of 100x = 72x Selling price of article to customer B after two successive discounts = 85% of 85% of 100x = 72.25x According to question- 72.25x - 72x = 4 0.25x = 4 x = 16 Selling price of article to customer B after two successive discounts = 72.25x = Rs.1156 Marked price of article = 100x = Rs.1600 Cost price of article = 1600 * (100/160) = Rs.1000 Profit amount when the article is sold to B = 1156 - 1000 = Rs.156

questions.question: Find the value of x, y and z, so as to satisfy the equations given below. x + y + z = 8, x - yz = -71, x - y + z = -22
What is the value of: √[-5 + 3√3]_________√[5 + 3√3] + √[(-1)(1 + √3)]_________√(1 - √3)
questions.explanation: Here, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230220t2lter-002.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230220t2lter-003.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230220t2lter-004.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230220t2lter-005.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230220t2lter-006.png)

questions.question: The length of the minute hand of a clock is 12 cm. What is the distance travelled by the tip of the minute hand of the clock in 15 min?
questions.explanation: Perimeter of a circle = 2πR Since the minute hand travels one complete circle in 60 minutes hence in 15 minutes it will travel ¼ of the circle. Hence total distance covered = 2πR/4 = 2π x 12/4 = 6π cm

questions.question: X and Y together can finish a job in 12 days. They start together but after 3 days X stops; Y takes 15 more days to finish. How many days would Y alone take to do the job?
questions.explanation: Let total work = 1 Work done by X and Y in one day = 1/12 Work done by X and Y in 3 days = 3/12 = ¼ Remaining work = 1 - (¼) = ¾ This will be done by Y in 15 days. So, Y alone complete the whole work in 15 x 4/3 = 20 days

questions.question: The perpendicular length from the origin to the line 8X + 6Y - 98 = 0
questions.explanation: Given point (0, 0) and line 8X + 6Y - 98 = 0 Here, A = 8, B = 6, C = -98, X₁ = 0, Y₁ = 0 Distance = (|AX₁ + BY₂ + C|)/√(A² + B²) = (|8 * 0 + 6 * 0 - 98|)/√(8² + 6²) = 98/10 = 9.8 square units

questions.question: The number of people in building A, building B, and Building C is 432. If the ratio of the number of people in building A to that in building C is 9: 8 and the ratio of the number of people in building B and building C together to that in building A is 7/5, then the number of people in building B is:
questions.explanation: Let the number of people in building A and building C be 9a and 8a, respectively. Let the number of people in buildings B and C together and building A be 7b and 5b, respectively. A/Q 9a = 5b b = 9a/5 The number of people in building B = 7b - 8a = 7*9a/5 - 8a = 23a/5 The total people = 432 9a + 8a + 23a/5 = 432 85a + 23a = 2160 108a = 2160 a = 20 Hence, the required value = 23 * 20/5 = 92

questions.question: A pole stands vertically on the ground with the help of a 66-meter wire tied to its top and affixed to the ground. If the steel wire makes an angle of 30˚ with the horizontal ground, then the height of the pole is equal to:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29911/image001.png) AB (Length of wire) = 66 m AC = Height of pole AC/AB = sin 30˚ AC = ½ * 66 AC = 33 m

questions.question: Find the area of a triangle whose base is 18 cm and altitude to that base is 12 cm.
questions.explanation: Area of triangle = (½) X base X height = (½) X 18 X 12 = 9 X 12 = 108 cm²

questions.question: The average weight of 16 boxes increases by 2.5 gm, when another box A comes in place of one of them weighing 160 gm. Find the weight of box A.
questions.explanation: Weight of one of the boxes = 160 gm Increased average = 2.5 gm Weight of box A = 160 + 16 * 2.5 = 200 gm

questions.question: Vikki and Vibha save 50% and 40% respectively of their monthly income. If the monthly income of Vibha is 25% more than that of Vikki, the expenditure of Vibha is what percent more than that of Vikki?
questions.explanation: Let the income of Vikki = 100x Income of Vibha = 125x Savings of Vikki = 50% of 100x = 50x Expenditure of Vikki = 100x - 50x = 50x Savings of Vibha = 40% of 125x = 50x Expenditure of Vibha = 125x - 50x = 75x Required percentage = (75x - 50x)/50x * 100 = 50%

questions.question: What is the smallest integer that is divisible by 54, 45, and 72?
questions.explanation: L.C.M of 54, 45, and 72 = 1080 Hence, the required number = 1080

questions.question: Which of the following can be classified as an onomatopoeic expression?
questions.explanation: Buzz imitates the sound made by insects or machines, making it an onomatopoeic word. The other options express abstract ideas or emotions. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Note: Onomatopoeia is the formation or use of words that imitate the natural sounds they describe, like "buzz," "hiss," "bang," or "meow," making writing more vivid and engaging by letting readers "hear" the action. It's a literary device where the word's sound mimics the actual sound, adding fun and realism to descriptions of animal noises, impacts, or water sounds.

questions.question: Pick the spelling that is correct for a state of mental calmness.
questions.explanation: "Equanimity" means "a state of mental calmness, composure, and evenness of temper, especially in difficult situations". Usage: She faced the unexpected challenges with remarkable equanimity and grace. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct antonym of "INSALUBRIOUS".
questions.explanation: "Insalubrious" means unhealthy. (a) Wholesome = healthy (b) Toxic = poisonous (c) Noxious = harmful (d) Putrid = decaying Only (a) is opposite. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: What does the "House of Brands" model primarily involve, as per the passage?
options: One global brand identity for all the outlets
questions.explanation: The passage states, "a parent company owns and manages multiple independent brands, each with a distinct identity, positioning, and target audience." This implies that several standalone brands function separately while being controlled by a single parent company. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the House of Brands model, the parent brand is usually:
questions.explanation: The passage states, "the House of Brands model keeps the parent company largely in the background." This indicates that the parent brand does not dominate consumer perception and allows individual brands to take centre stage. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Why is a single brand often ineffective today, as per the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage states, "Modern consumers are not a homogeneous group; their preferences vary widely based on age, income, lifestyle, and values." This suggests that one brand cannot credibly cater to such varied consumer segments. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What does 'risk mitigation' imply in the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage states, "Another significant advantage of the House of Brands strategy is risk mitigation. When brands operate independently, the failure or reputational damage of one does not necessarily affect the others." This implies that negative outcomes related to one brand are contained and do not spill over to the entire portfolio. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: 'Mamaearth' is positioned mainly as a:
questions.explanation: The passage states, "Mamaearth initially positioned itself as a natural and toxin-free brand appealing to families." This indicates a focus on natural products and family-oriented values rather than clinical or luxury positioning. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a word that is spelled incorrectly?
options: The theory attempts to reconcile opposing viewpoints.
Her response was marked by intellectual subtlety.
The historian provided a chronological acount of events.
The research methodology was carefully designed.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, (c) is incorrect because the word "acount" is misspelt. The correct spelling is "account", meaning a detailed description or record. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. Only the concerted efforts of the Government and the agencies involved in disaster management can help them lead normal lives again. Q. Natural calamities generally bring death and disaster on a large scale. R. Tsunami, waves, earthquakes, floods, droughts and epidemics have taken a heavy toll of human lives, particularly the children. S. The children who survive these disasters suffer the most.
questions.explanation: P cannot be the introductory sentence as it uses the pronoun 'them' which requires prior context. Thus, we can eliminate options b) and d). With Q as the introductory sentence, R should follow as it gives examples of the various disasters that bring large-scale destruction. R indicates that these disasters adversely affect the children in particular. So, sentence S should follow which sheds more light on this topic. Sentence P can be a suitable conclusion as it tells us that concerted efforts of disaster management agencies can help these children lead normal lives again. Therefore, the correct sequence is QRSP (option a)).

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. One day the donkey suddenly tumbled down the stream and the salt bag also fell into the water. B. A salt seller used to carry the salt bag on his donkey to the market every day. C. The salt dissolved in the water and hence the bag became very light to carry. D. On the way they had to cross a stream.
questions.explanation: Both sentences 'A' and 'B' seem fit to be the first sentence in the passage. But, on reading all the sentences, it is understood that AC is a mandatory pair. Sentence 'A' mentions the donkey tumbling down the stream and the salt bag falling into the water, and 'C' discusses its effect on the weight of the salt bag. The only option with AC as a pair is option a). So, B starts the passage and introduces the subjects of the story 'Salt seller and his donkey'. D follows and informs us about their route. And AC informs of the incident that took place one day while they were following the same route. Therefore, the correct order is BDAC. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. There is a lot of ground to cover today.
options: There has been a lot of ground to be covered today.
Today we have to cover a lot of ground.
There is a lot of ground to be covered that day.
There is a lot of ground to be covered today.
questions.explanation: · The correct answer is (d) There is a lot of ground to be covered today. · The subject of the Active Voice is changed into object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed is changed into subject of the Passive Voice. · The usage of a helping verb is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. · Preposition "by" is used before the object made in Passive Voice. However, it is optional in some cases. · The question in Active voice is in Infinitive form. · Infinitive sentence is: To + V1 form. · The format in Active voice is: There + to be + Noun + Infinitive. · Thus, the format in Passive voice will be: There + to be + Noun + To be + V3. · For example: o There is a lot of work to do. (Active) o There is a lot of work to be done. (Passive) · The options except (d) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. · Therefore, the correct answer is: There is a lot of ground to be covered today. V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s)

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. Jack said to me, "I'm going to seek my own fortune."
options: Jack told me that I was going to seek his own fortune.
Jack told me that he was going to seek my own fortune.
Jack told me that he is going to seek his own fortune.
Jack told me that he was going to seek his own fortune.
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Direct Speech is in Present Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Indirect Speech should be in Past Continuous Tense. Present Continuous (Subject + is/am/are + V1 + ing + Object) should be converted into Past Continuous (Subject + was/were + V1 + ing + Object). For example: He said, "I am checking the assignment." (Direct Speech) He said that he was checking the assignment. (Indirect Speech) The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: · The reporting verb 'said to' is changed into 'told'. · The pronoun 'I' is changed into 'he' and the possessive determiner 'my' is changed into 'his'. · The verb 'am going' (present continuous) is changed into 'was going' (past continuous). Therefore, the correct answer is option (d): Jack told me that he was going to seek his own fortune.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate phrasal verb to fill in the blank. I need to _____ all the clutter in my room before the guests arrive.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, 'get rid of' fits appropriately in the blank as it means to remove or dispose of something unwanted or unnecessary which aligns perfectly with the sentence describing the need to remove all the clutter in the room before the guests arrive. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. The final sentence: I need to get rid of all the clutter in my room before the guests arrive. Look forward to: To anticipate something with pleasure or excitement. Make up for: To compensate for something or to do something to balance a previous action or situation. Keep up with: To maintain the same pace or level as something or someone else. Note: Phrasal verb: A phrase that consists of a verb with a preposition or adverb or both, the meaning of which is different from the meaning of its separate parts. Example: She has always looked down on me. Here, the phrasal verb to look down on someone doesnt mean that you are looking down from a higher place at someone who is below you; it means that you think that you are better than someone.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Nitin and Usha got married ____ after their engagement was finalised.
questions.explanation: The given sentence talks about how Nitin and Usha tied the knot after their engagement was fixed. Among the given options, 'quickly' fits in the blank appropriately as its usage conveys that Nitin and Usha got married with all possible haste once the engagement was finalised. Complete sentence: Nitin and Usha got married quickly after their engagement was finalised. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. The rest of the options render the sentence meaningless.

questions.question: Choose the correct direct speech form of the following sentence. The operations head said that the logistics team would have finalized all shipment schedules before the festive season began.
options: The operations head said, "The logistics team has finalized all shipment schedules before the festive season began."
The operations head told, "The logistics team should have finalized all shipment schedules before the festive season began."
The operations head said, "The logistics team will have finalized all shipment schedules before the festive season begins."
The operations head said, "The logistics team finalizes all shipment schedules before the festive season begins."
questions.explanation: The sentence in Indirect Speech is in the Perfect Conditional tense: "would have finalized". When converting to Direct Speech, Perfect Conditional changes to Future Perfect. Perfect Conditional: would + have + V3 Future Perfect: will + have + V3 The following changes occur when converting from Indirect to Direct Speech: Reporting verb "said" remains unchanged Tense change: "would have finalized" → "will have finalized" Time reference is adjusted appropriately for Direct Speech Inverted commas are added Therefore, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The detective was determined to capture the nefarious mastermind behind the series of bank robberies.
questions.explanation: Nefarious: Wicked, villainous, or criminal in nature. Virtuous: Having or showing high moral standards; righteous. Wicked: Evil by nature; morally wrong or bad. Evil: Morally wrong or intending to harm or cause misfortune. Villainous: Relating to or characteristic of a villain; wicked. Among the given options, the word 'virtuous' is opposite in meaning to the word 'nefarious'. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the option that will replace the error highlighted in bold. All members of the committee were willing to except the proposal accept the chairperson, who raised ethical concerns.
questions.explanation: Except means excluding. Accept means to agree to or receive. Thus, except the chairperson and accept the proposal are correct. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the sentence that uses the given idiom correctly. A snowball effect
options: He had a snowball effect when he rolled down a hill covered with snow.
She had a snowball effect when she started a chain reaction with her actions.
I had a snowball effect when I made some snowballs with my friends.
They had a snowball effect when they experienced some cold weather.
questions.explanation: The idiom "a snowball effect" means a situation in which something small or insignificant causes something else that is bigger or more important to happen, and then that thing causes another thing that is even bigger or more important to happen, and so on. It usually has a neutral or negative connotation. Option (b) uses the idiom correctly, as it implies that she triggered a series of events that grew in size or impact. The other options either use the idiom in a literal sense, which is not appropriate or in a way that does not match the meaning of the idiom, which is confusing. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Every tree grows/ in it's own unique way,/ contributing to the beauty/ and diversity of the forest.
questions.explanation: The part that contains the error is 'in it's own unique way'. The error lies in the incorrect use of the contraction "it's". The word "it's" is a contraction of "it is" or "it has". However, in the given sentence, the intended meaning is to indicate possession, which requires the possessive form "its". "Its" is a possessive determiner used to indicate that something belongs to or is associated with something previously mentioned. In the sentence, "its" indicates possession, showing that each tree has its own specific and unique way of growing. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: Every tree grows in its own unique way, contributing to the beauty and diversity of the forest. NOTE: Possessive Determiners/Adjectives: Words like 'my', 'our', 'your', 'his', 'her', 'its', and 'their' are known as possessive adjectives (or possessive determiners). They come before nouns and indicate ownership of the noun. Example: "The teacher asked the students to open their drawing books."

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Being accustomed to work/ methodically, I tried at first to arrange/ the photographs in/ order of importance.
options: methodically, I tried at first to arrange
questions.explanation: There are many expressions, such as 'looking forward to', 'accustomed to', 'averse to', 'object to', 'prior to', etc., that are commonly followed by a gerund. So, the use of 'working' is appropriate here. Hence, option (c) is correct. Corrected sentence: Being accustomed to working methodically, I tried at first to arrange the photographs in order of importance. NOTE/- Gerund: Gerund is a verb form that functions as a noun, ending in -ing. A gerund can be either the subject or object of a sentence in English. Example: "Everybody enjoyed her dancing and singing at the party." The words 'dancing' and 'singing' are gerunds.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who has an unnatural anxiety about one's own health typically involving imagined.
questions.explanation: Option (b) is the correct answer. Ascetic - One who leads an austere life Chauvinist - A person who is blindly devoted to an idea/ a person displaying aggressive or exaggerated patriotism Fatalist - One who believes in fate

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word Prodigal?
options: A person who saves money cautiously
A person who invests money wisely
A person who spends money extravagantly
A person who earns money slowly
questions.explanation: Prodigal means wastefully extravagant, especially in spending money. Therefore, option (c) is correct. The remaining options suggest careful saving or earning, which are the opposite of the word's meaning.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "PERFIDIOUS".
questions.explanation: "Perfidious" means deceitful or treacherous. (a) Faithful = loyal (b) Loyal = faithful (c) Treacherous = traitorous (d) Devout = deeply religious Only (c) is synonymous. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrasal verb in the given sentence. Despite the uncertainty, they decided to roll the dice and invest in the promising startup.
questions.explanation: The idiom "roll the dice" means to take a gamble. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.
